extracted_information: The WEB CONTENT details comprehensive container escape prevention techniques and detection methods, as published on April 20, 2025. Prevention focuses on secure container configurations (Dockerfile best practices, Kubernetes runtime security contexts), network hardening, kernel-level security, and enhanced container runtime security. Detection involves runtime security monitoring using tools like Falco, custom Python and Bash scripts for active scanning, and host-level monitoring for suspicious activities.

methods: Create non-root users early in the build process (`ADDGROUP`, `ADDUSER`)
Install dependencies as root, then switch to a non-root user
Remove unnecessary packages and temporary files (`apk del --purge`, `rm -rf /var/cache/apk/* /tmp/*`)
Switch to a non-root user (`USER appuser`, `USER 1001:1001`)
Drop all capabilities (`capabilities: drop: - ALL`) and add only necessary ones (`add: - NET_BIND_SERVICE`)
Use restricted seccomp profile (`seccompProfile: type: RuntimeDefault`)
Restrict volume mounts (`readOnly: true` for sensitive data)

techniques.name: Network Security Hardening (Kubernetes NetworkPolicy)
details: Restrict ingress traffic from specific namespaces/labels and ports (e.g., allow from `api-gateway` on port 8080)
Restrict egress traffic to specific namespaces/labels and ports (e.g., allow to `database` on port 5432)

methods: Apply AppArmor/SELinux profiles for containers to deny dangerous capabilities (`sys_admin`, `sys_module`, `sys_rawio`) and sensitive areas (`/proc/sys`, `/sys`, `/dev/mem`, `/dev/kmem`)
Use gVisor (`runtimeClassName: gvisor`, `handler: runsc`) for enhanced container isolation

details.condition: spawned processes `chroot`, `nsenter`, `unshare` OR command line contains `mount /dev`, `/proc/1/root`, `docker.sock`

details.description: Detect containers running with dangerous privileges
Detect access to sensitive host files from containers
details.condition: file descriptor name starts with `/proc/1/root/` OR is `/etc/shadow` OR `/etc/passwd` OR starts with `/var/run/docker.sock`

functions: Check running containers for dangerous configurations

Analyze individual container for security issues (privileged mode, dangerous volume mounts like `/`, `/proc`, `/var/run/docker.sock`, dangerous capabilities like `SYS_ADMIN`, `ALL`, host network mode)
Report detected security issues and integrate with SIEM/alerting systems

monitoring_areas: Unauthorized mount operations (recent `dmesg` entries for `mount`, current mounts indicating escape like `/proc/1/root`, `/var/run/docker.sock`)
System calls indicating potential escape (`auditctl` for `mount`, `unshare`, `setns` syscalls)
Network monitoring for unusual container traffic or host network access (`netstat`, `ss` for unexpected ports or host network usage)
